column1: # Curated monetary-base / broad-money figures for the top fiat
# Refresh cadence: quarterly. Each central bank publishes an
# authoritative monthly or quarterly aggregate; the source column
# below identifies the exact series so an operator can refresh
# Methodology: we use M2 (broad money: currency + checking +
# savings + small time deposits) wherever the central bank
# publishes it under that name. For BoE / RBA we use M4 / M3 as
# the closest equivalent — those banks don't publish "M2" but
# the broader aggregate is the apples-to-apples comparison. For
# CNY M2 we follow PBoC's domestic M2.
# `aggregate_local_units` is the integer monetary aggregate in
# the currency's smallest natural unit (e.g. dollars for USD
# euros for EUR — NOT cents). `as_of` is the data-vintage date
# the central bank stamped on the series. `source` is a free-text
# series identifier callers can paste into the central bank's
# A row's absence yields NULL `circulating_supply` + NULL
# Coverage today: ~25 currencies covering >95% of global fx
# spot volume (BIS Triennial Survey 2022 weights). Expanding
# to "all" via the World Bank API (FM.LBL.BMNY.CN
# countries) is a follow-up — Eurozone members and currency
# unions need special handling because the WB series isn't
# Lines starting with `#` are comments. Trailing whitespace OK.
